mission.title: perses: [FEATURE] New TracingGanttChart panel
mission.description:
# Description
This PR adds a new `TracingGanttChart` panel. This is built from scratch, based on React and Material UI.

Resolves #978
Obsoletes #2033

**Features**
* colors are generated deterministically (same as the `TimeSeriesChart`)
* spans with errors have a red hue and an error icon next to the name
* span collapsing
* span indention guide highlighting
* resizable divider (between span name and span bars)
* dynamic indention width based on the spans in viewport (making it easier to navigate deeply nested spans)
* ticks at 0%, 25%, 50%, 75% and 100%
* mini Gantt chart overview to select a time frame of the trace to zoom into
* sidepanel: span attributes and events are visible in a side panel (after clicking on a span)
* performance: tested with traces up to 800 spans; `react-virtuoso` only renders rows which are visible
* unit tests

**Alternative Gantt chart libraries considered**
* https://scheduler.bitnoise.pl/
* https://frappe.io/gantt
* https://github.com/MaTeMaTuK/gantt-tas

resolution.summary:
# Description

This PR adds a new `TracingGanttChart` panel, based on the Jaeger UI Gantt chart.

I'm currently working on:
- [ ] switch from Jaeger to OpenTelemetry data model (https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/otel/overview/#tracing-signal)
- [ ] fix the selection in the mini Gantt chart and the draggable divider (between span names and gantt chart)

### Open questions

**UI/UX**
We need to update the explore page to show a table of all matched traces/spans. After clicking on a trace, the Gantt chart should show up. Currently the Gantt Chart is embedded in a panel and occupies only half the browser's height because of the query editors on top - ideally there should be more space for the Gantt chart (in Jaeger UI, it's displayed fullscreen).

**CSS**
Jaeger UI uses global CSS files. This can be a problem when other Perses components use the same `id`s or CSS classes. Ideally we'll switch to CSS modules.
Currently I'm `rsync`'ing the css files to the `dist` folder in the `build:css` npm script. This is a problem however when embedding components (see https://github.com/perses/perses/issues/894) - ideally the CSS gets compiled and embedded in the JS files in the `dist` folder of the plugin. This means we need a webpack step for each (or only this?) plugin :|.

**Styling**
Jaeger UI uses the Ant Design Library, Perses uses Material UI. Probably we should replace all components with their Material UI counterparts.
Additionally, it should be possible to customize the styling wh
steps: Review the issue discussion for context
Apply the fix from the linked pull request
